our_open_deliverables.text: Return the final cleaned SOW with accepted scope changes for Omnia's internal sign-off
Send Marianne the Dun & Bradstreet enrichment proposal (parent-child linkage, diversity status, USD 10-25k/yr) plus the final MSA and Order 1
Provide Dieter the Swagger API link and endpoint specs for the integration alignment

buyer_dependent_items.text: Complete internal legal cover / Adobe Sign audit trail and return signed MSA and Order 1 for Zycus countersignature
Share Omnia's integration-standards framework and OIB endpoint specs with the Zycus delivery team
Send the Worldcheck One / Inoxico sample PDF reports and the fields/output needed to scope verification-agency apps

competitive_position.summary: No active competitive threat in contracting: this is a sole-source finish. Coupa (eliminated on cost/complexity, 2024) and iValua (eliminated on weaker CLM, 2024) are both out; the displaced incumbent is Omnia's manual Excel/Worldcheck process and scattered file-store contracts; Adobe Sign is being replaced by Certinal. The only competitive-adjacent live item is Omnia replacing implementation partner Letsema, where Zycus is positioning to take change management in-house. Strongest current 'threat' is execution risk (SOW scope, internal legal routing), not a rival.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Coupa is strong in spend management but expensive and complex for our multi-entity Africa/APAC rollout.
competitors.source: Prior record: Marianne, 17 Oct 2024 discovery; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Native iContract CLM depth plus Merlin contract discovery at a competitive multi-entity price; already selected.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: iValua has procurement focus but weaker contract lifecycle capabilities.
competitors.source: Prior record: Marianne, 17 Oct 2024 discovery; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Stronger contract lifecycle and clause-level version control; already selected.

competitors.name: Manual Excel / Worldcheck / do-nothing (incumbent process)
competitors.quote: As we're having to do it manually now, it's one of the things that's quite painful for me.
competitors.how_we_win: Automate supplier screening and spend analytics; centralize 6,000 contracts into one iContract repository.

competitors.name: Adobe Sign (e-signature incumbent)
competitors.quote: Post reviews it will be Certinal... also workable in China.
competitors.how_we_win: Certinal e-signature is scoped in the SOW and enforceable across Omnia geographies including China.

competitors.name: Letsema (implementation partner being replaced)
competitors.quote: Omnia is looking to replace Letsema as the implementation partner on this programme.
competitors.source: SF task 2026-06-30 (Validation of Implementation Partner Scope)
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus offers native change management and PMO so Omnia can avoid a costly external SI; validate partner scope directly.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole + Avoma 23 Jun and 30 Jun 2026 + SF LastActivityDate 01 Jul 2026
champion_strength.summary: Marianne Ventura is a strong, active champion driving the deal to signature, emotionally invested at close and controlling Omnia's internal legal-cover routing, with Dieter Sparbier a credible second advocate on the technical side. Engagement is continuous through the 01 Jul LastActivityDate with no cooling.
champion_strength.champion: Marianne Ventura, Senior Category Manager: IT & Communications

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Stephan Serfontein (Group Financial Director), Jacques De Villiers (Group: Head of Supply Chain)
Avoma 30 Jun 2026 (scope rise to USD 1bn, price held flat)
Prior record: 19 Feb 2026 SOW review commercial approval
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Stephan Serfontein (Group Financial Director) and Jacques De Villiers (Group: Head of Supply Chain), both Salesforce contact roles, with the USD 234k commercial approved during the 19 Feb 2026 SOW review. At this contracting stage the commercial envelope is settled: on 30 Jun Zycus confirmed the USD 900m-to-USD 1bn scope rise attracts no additional charge, removing the last budget question. The EBs are currently passive (neither appears on the 17 Jun, 23 Jun or 30 Jun calls, which Marianne and legal drove), which is normal for a late-stage legal close; the live commercial-sign figure is instead being carried by Marianne and Tracy's legal cover. No budget risk is evident.

recommended_moves: Agree a dated signing plan with Marianne: internal legal cover, Adobe Sign, then Zycus countersignature.
Send Marianne the D&B enrichment proposal and the final MSA and Order 1 in one clean email.
Hold the integration-standards session with Dieter and exchange Swagger specs and Omnia's OIB framework.
